disease.name: Infectious Colitis (GI Bleeding)
disease.note: Bacterial enterocolitis presenting with bloody diarrhea/hematochezia. Key pathogens: Shigella, Salmonella, Campylobacter jejuni, E. coli O157:H7 (EHEC), Yersinia, C. difficile, Entamoeba histolytica. EHEC carries risk of HUS. C. diff can progress to toxic megacolon.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: Toxic megacolon/sepsis/HUS -> EMERGENCY; mild colitis -> supportive care

subtypes.name: Shigellosis (Shigella)
subtypes.notes: High fever, tenesmus, small-volume bloody stools, fecal leukocytes

subtypes.name: Campylobacter Enterocolitis
subtypes.notes: Frequently precedes Guillain-Barre, severe abdominal pain

subtypes.name: EHEC O157:H7
subtypes.notes: Hemorrhagic colitis, risk of HUS, NO antibiotics

secondLine.test: CT abdomen with IV contrast (colonic wall thickening, pancolitis)
Flexible sigmoidoscopy with biopsy (C. diff pseudomembranes, CMV if immunocompromised)
Free light chains / IgA (HUS workup if atypical)

investigations.goldStandard: Stool culture plus molecular testing (PCR) for pathogen identification

emergency: Aggressive IV fluids and electrolyte repletion
IV antibiotics if severe/immunocompromised (ceftriaxone + metronidazole for severe)
Oral vancomycin (125mg QID) or fidaxomicin for C. diff
inpatient: Supportive care - IV fluids, electrolyte monitoring
Antibiotics for Shigella (azithromycin), Campylobacter (azithromycin), Salmonella (if severe/immunocompromised)
AVOID antimotility agents (loperamide) - increase toxin retention
AVOID antibiotics for EHEC - increase HUS risk
outpatient: Follow-up stool culture to document clearance (Salmonella/Shigella)
preventive: Antibiotic stewardship to prevent C. diff

disease.pathophysiology: Pathogen invades colonic mucosa (Shigella, Salmonella, Campylobacter, E. coli, amoeba) → inflammatory response → mucosal edema, ulceration, microabscesses → friable mucosa bleeds → hematochezia. EHEC produces Shiga toxin → endothelial damage → hemorrhagic colitis + risk of HUS. C. diff toxins A/B cause tight junction disruption, neutrophil infiltration, and pseudomembrane formation.
mustNotMiss: EHEC: NO antibiotics (increase HUS risk), monitor for HUS (thrombocytopenia, microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, AKI)
C. diff: NO antimotility agents, contact isolation, oral vancomycin is superior to metronidazole for severe
Toxic megacolon: abdominal XR for colonic diameter >6cm, immediate surgical consult
First episode of colitis may be IBD, not infectious - colonoscopy with biopsy if recurrent/atypical

diagnosticClues.clue: Acute bloody diarrhea + fever + recent food/travel/antibiotic exposure
Positive stool culture/PCR for specific pathogen

exclusionClues.clue: Chronic symptoms with growth failure, extra-intestinal manifestations (IBD)
Negative stool cultures and C. diff toxin
Watershed distribution of colitis on CT (ischemic)
